title: pinguicula filifolia subsp. alba
intro: Pinguicula filifolia subsp. alba, also known as the White Thread-leaved Butterwort, is a rare and unique carnivorous plant species belonging to the Pinguicula genus. The White Thread-leaved Butterwort has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been reported to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as protecting against bacterial and fungal infections, and has been used in herbal preparations to support the immune system and reduce stress. In traditional herbal medicine, the White Thread-leaved Butterwort is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
